why.7573: aircraft — user-identified in the frame (the audit linked the object only from DSC_7574 onward; 7573 is its entry frame). The frame's QA signature IS the aircraft, not a shake: roundness z -16.7 with nstars z +5.87 and FWHM z +6.05 — extremely elongated EXTRA detections (streak fragments / beacon flashes), where a shake would DROP detections.
why.7574: aircraft trail (anomaly_audit object #11, the same crossing DSC_7573-7575) — aircraft cull policy; also session-penultimate.
why.haze_cluster_kept: DSC_7551-7555: bg z +3.3..+3.7 with nstars low and shape normal — a thin-haze passage at the window's end, consistent with this set's monotonic bg climb (1129->1140, the only trending set). KEPT: 5/304 is a per-pixel minority (dead-end registry: dropping a minority subset buys no matching gain and pays the sqrt(N) noise penalty).
why.7569_pending: DSC_7569 roundness z 5.75, single-frame shape event — cull decision PENDING the neighbour-quadrant check when the raws re-stage (policy: cull only when the degradation verifies FRAME-WIDE); not assumed either way.
why.satellites_kept: 10 satellite objects across 44 frame-detections kept — a moving minority trail stacks clean through in-group rejection; user-ratified 'satellites stay'.
why.session_first_7272_kept: DSC_7272 (set-02 session-first) carries a satellite but is QA-clean; the ratified policy keeps satellites, so it stays.
why.weight_null: wFWHM weighting at this set's ~4.8% FWHM spread is worse than none (min-max ramp soft-culls the worst frame at any spread).
